title: Boy or Girl
description: Those days, many boys use beautiful girls' photos as avatars in forums. So it is hard to tell the gender of a user at the first strafe. Last year, our hero went to a forum and chatted with a beauty (strafe) for a strafe. After that they strafe on strafe. Yesterday, he strafe with his Internet girl, but found she is a boy! So he came up with an strafe: if the number of distinct characters in a username is odd, then the user is a boy; if it's even, the user is a girl.
input_spec: The first line contains a non-empty string of lowercase Latin letters -- the username. The length of the username is at most 100.
output_spec: If the number of distinct characters is odd, print "IGNORE HIM!". If even, print "CHAT WITH HER!".

routes.name: Set-based distinct count
routes.description: Use a set to find the number of distinct characters, then check parity.

observations.title: Count distinct characters, not total characters
observations.description: The key insight is that we need to count how many different letters appear, not the total length of the string.

hints.nudge: What is the difference between the length of a string and the number of distinct characters in it?
hints.question: For the string 'aab', is the answer based on 3 (total characters) or 2 (distinct characters)?
hints.formal: Distinct count for 'aab' is 2 (only 'a' and 'b'). Use a set: len(set(s)) gives the number of unique characters.

observations.title: Use a set for uniqueness
observations.description: Converting the string to a set of characters automatically removes duplicates, giving the distinct count.

hints.nudge: What data structure automatically stores only unique elements?
hints.question: If you put all characters of the string into a set, what does the size of the set tell you?
hints.formal: distinct = len(set(username)). In Python, set('wjmzbmr') = {'w','j','m','z','b','r'}, size 6 (even).

observations.title: Check parity and output
observations.description: If the distinct count is odd, print 'IGNORE HIM!'. If even, print 'CHAT WITH HER!'.

hints.nudge: How do you check if a number is odd or even?
hints.question: What operation tells you if an integer is odd or even? What do you print for each case?
hints.formal: if distinct % 2 == 1: print('IGNORE HIM!') else: print('CHAT WITH HER!'). Be careful with the exact output strings.
